rationale: The page contains significant educational content regarding the constitutional roles and duties of the Speaker and Deputy Speaker of the Lok Sabha, including historical background and relevant Articles (94, 95, 110). The bottom half is a promotional advertisement which has been excluded from the cleaned text.
cleanedText:
Core Duties of the Speaker
* Rights Guardian: Ensures the protection of rights and privileges within the House.
* Regulatory Authority: Manages matters not covered by specific rules.
* Deadlock Resolver: Uses voting power to break ties in parliamentary decisions.
* Joint Session Presider: Oversees joint meetings of both Houses of Parliament.
* Money Bill Authority Under Article 110 (4): Determines the status of financial bills.
About Deputy Speaker (DS) of Lok Sabha
* Background: Office emerged in 1921 under Government of India Act, 1919.
* Sachidananda Sinha first held this office in Central Legislative Assembly.
* M. A. Ayyangar became first elected DS post-independence.
* Election: As per a long-standing convention, post of DS has been offered to Opposition.
* As per Rules of Procedure and Conduct of Business in Lok Sabha, DS election shall be held on such date as Speaker may fix.
* Resignation and Removal: Article 94 deals with vacation, resignation and removal (by resolution passed by majority of all then members of House).
* Speaker may resign by submitting a resignation to DS and vice-versa.
* Duties: As per Article 95, DS performs duties of Speaker in case of vacancy, absence, etc.
* DS has same power as of Speaker when presiding over sitting of House and no appeal lies against his rulings.
